subject: Abstract expressionist rendering of a vast, barely-discernible entity — a large eye-like form amid swirling blue-grey brushstrokes suggesting an immense creature seen from close range
description: Drew Tucker's loose gestural brushwork fills the entire frame with swirling teal, cyan, grey, and white strokes. Near the upper center, a large circular form suggests a massive eye or hollow socket. Faint suggestion of a large head or skull shape beneath the eye. The strokes spiral and sweep in arcs that evoke immense scale and motion. There is no clear horizon, no environment, no secondary figures — only the overwhelming mass of the implied entity and the turbulence of its presence. The style is expressive and deliberately illegible at fine detail.
foreground: Swirling teal-grey brushstrokes suggesting a vast face or eye at close range
background: Indistinguishable from foreground — continuous abstract field
